hard_test_inputs/hard_basal.csv.note: Blended 72% Basal + 28% Her2 signal. Should predict Basal but Her2 probability will be elevated (~15-35%).
hard_test_inputs/hard_basal.csv.hardness: HARD — Basal/Her2 is the most clinically confused pair

hard_test_inputs/hard_her2.csv.note: Blended 68% Her2 + 32% LumB. LumB probability likely 15-40%. Classic HER2+/HR+ borderline case.
hard_test_inputs/hard_her2.csv.hardness: HARD — Her2/LumB confusion is common in HER2+/HR+ tumors

hard_test_inputs/hard_luma.csv.note: 65% LumA + 35% Normal. The most similar subtype pair biologically. Normal probability 20-45% expected.
hard_test_inputs/hard_luma.csv.hardness: VERY HARD — LumA/Normal have near-identical expression profiles

hard_test_inputs/hard_lumb.csv.note: 67% LumB + 33% LumA. The #1 confusion pair in your confusion matrix. LumA probability 15-40%.
hard_test_inputs/hard_lumb.csv.hardness: VERY HARD — Confirmed most frequent misclassification in your test results

hard_test_inputs/hard_normal.csv.note: 70% Normal + 30% LumA. Normal-like is the weakest class (35% F1 in your test). May fail entirely.
hard_test_inputs/hard_normal.csv.hardness: EXTREME — Normal had only 35% F1. This is the model's genuine weakness.
